meaning: British Medical Journal <<英国医学杂志>>
examples: I agree with the BMJ research, not the WHO.
There are no other known cases of mishaps beyond the two the BMJ has detailed.
Writing in the BMJ, the authors said their findings have important health implications.
Now, a new study published in BMJ provides further information about this area.
The study published on the BMJ website said patients taking Actos had fewer heart problems.
